features.summary: Tesla reported record Q1 2021 results with over $1 billion in non-GAAP net income for the first time, despite facing severe supply chain challenges including semiconductor shortages and COVID-related restrictions. The company successfully launched Model Y production in Shanghai with positive gross margins in the first quarter. However, the transition to new Model S/X production created headwinds with an estimated $200 million P&L impact and no customer deliveries during Q1. Tesla invested $1.5 billion in Bitcoin and subsequently sold 10% of the position for a small gain.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Model S deliveries to begin in May with production ramping to over 2,000 units per week by Q3, while Model X deliveries are planned for Q3. The company maintains its long-term growth target of 50% annually and expects to exceed that in 2021. Austin and Berlin factories are progressing well with initial limited production expected this year and volume production next year. Tesla anticipates doubling supplier battery cell capacity next year and achieving volume production of 4680 cells within 12-18 months.
features.risks: The company faces ongoing global supply chain instability, particularly around semiconductors and port capacity, leading to high expedite costs and minor production interruptions. Model S/X production ramp presents execution challenges with complex new technology integration. Solar Roof installations remain constrained by complexity assessment errors and installation bottlenecks. The transition from upfront FSD purchases to subscription model could temporarily reduce cash flows before building recurring revenue.
features.key_drivers: Strong global demand significantly exceeds production capacity across all vehicle lines, with Model 3 becoming the best-selling luxury sedan globally. The shift to solar-plus-Powerwall bundled installations should dramatically improve scalability by standardizing electrical configurations. Full Self-Driving development continues advancing with pure vision approach and expanding beta program. Energy storage business shows potential for vehicle-comparable margins as Powerwall matures, while Megapack requires additional cost optimization work.
